frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Dekho, main samajhta hoon ki har rishte mein adjustments hoti hain, but aajkal ke time mein shaadi karna mardon ke liye ek risk ban gaya hai. Yeh jo laws hain na alimony aur divorce ke, they're so skewed. Kahin-kahin toh lagta hai jaise yeh modern-day dowry hai, bas reverse mein. Kuch ladkiyan bas financial security ke liye rishta banati hain, aur phir chhote se dispute pe seedha case kar deti hain. Bechare mard phans jaate hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment frames marriage as a 'risk for men' due to 'skewed' alimony and divorce laws, explicitly calling it 'modern-day dowry, reverse mein.' It suggests some women marry solely for 'financial security' and misuse the legal system, directly encoding the 'women as golddiggers' and 'alimony as reverse dowry' manifestations.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Ajay. Age 38. A Rajput man working as a senior government officer in Jaipur. You are sitting in your office with the door closed talking to your junior male colleague after a female officer in your department submitted a formal request for work from home during her periods citing the new government circular. You are commenting on whether such requests should be entertained in a government office setting and what it means for discipline and productivity.
